AML Somatic Mutation Landscape — TCGA-LAML Cohort

Maria Antonietta Diana

2026-06-25

Dataset

This analysis examines the somatic mutation landscape of Acute Myeloid Leukaemia (AML) using open-access data from The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA-LAML cohort, n=153 patients).

Somatic mutation calls were downloaded from the GDC Data Portal (masked somatic mutations, Mutect2 ensemble calls, hg38). Individual per-sample MAF files were merged using scripts/part2_aml_cohort/03_merge_maf_files.py.

Setup

library(maftools)
library(tidyverse)
library(RColorBrewer)
maf <- read.maf("../data/processed/TCGA-LAML-merged.maf")
## -Reading
## -Validating
## -Silent variants: 988 
## -Summarizing
## --Mutiple centers found
## BI;WUGSC;BI;WUGSC--Possible FLAGS among top ten genes:
##   TTN
## -Processing clinical data
## --Missing clinical data
## -Finished in 0.176s elapsed (0.593s cpu)

Cohort Summary

plotmafSummary(maf,
               rmOutlier = TRUE,
               addStat   = "median",
               dashboard = TRUE,
               titvRaw   = FALSE)

Oncoprint

The oncoprint shows the most frequently mutated genes across the cohort. Each column is a patient, each row is a gene.

oncoplot(maf        = maf,
         top        = 20,
         removeNonMutated = TRUE)

Most Frequently Mutated AML Genes

gene_summary <- getGeneSummary(maf)

gene_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(AlteredSamples)) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  mutate(Gene = fct_reorder(Hugo_Symbol, AlteredSamples)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Gene, y = AlteredSamples / getSampleSummary(maf) %>% nrow() * 100)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2196F3", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 15 Mutated Genes in TCGA-LAML",
       x = NULL,
       y = "% Patients Mutated") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

Lollipop Plots — Key AML Drivers

lollipopPlot(maf     = maf,
             gene    = "FLT3",
             AACol   = "HGVSp_Short",
             showMutationRate = TRUE,
             labelPos = "all")

lollipopPlot(maf     = maf,
             gene    = "NPM1",
             AACol   = "HGVSp_Short",
             showMutationRate = TRUE)
##      HGNC    refseq.ID   protein.ID aa.length
##    <char>       <char>       <char>     <num>
## 1:   NPM1 NM_001037738 NP_001032827       259
## 2:   NPM1    NM_002520    NP_002511       294
## 3:   NPM1    NM_199185    NP_954654       265

lollipopPlot(maf     = maf,
             gene    = "DNMT3A",
             AACol   = "HGVSp_Short",
             showMutationRate = TRUE)
##      HGNC refseq.ID protein.ID aa.length
##    <char>    <char>     <char>     <num>
## 1: DNMT3A NM_022552  NP_072046       912
## 2: DNMT3A NM_153759  NP_715640       723
## 3: DNMT3A NM_175629  NP_783328       912

Mutational Signatures

maf_tnm <- trinucleotideMatrix(
  maf            = maf,
  ref_genome     = "BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38",
  ignoreChr      = "chrM",
  useSyn         = TRUE
)
plotSignatures(maf_tnm,
               title_size = 1.2)

Co-occurrence and Mutual Exclusivity

somaticInteractions(maf     = maf,
                    top     = 20,
                    pvalue  = c(0.05, 0.1))

##       gene1  gene2       pValue oddsRatio    00    01    11    10         pAdj
##      <char> <char>        <num>     <num> <int> <int> <int> <int>        <num>
##   1:  KMT2D HIVEP3 2.731158e-06       Inf   128     0     3     0 4.965743e-05
##   2:    TTN   PAN2 2.987205e-06       Inf   124     0     4     3 4.978674e-05
##   3: HIVEP3   PAN2 1.092463e-05       Inf   127     1     3     0 1.560662e-04
##   4:  KMT2D   PAN2 1.092463e-05       Inf   127     1     3     0 1.560662e-04
##   5: HIVEP3    TTN 9.559054e-05       Inf   124     4     3     0 1.194882e-03
##  ---                                                                          
## 186:   IDH1   KRAS 1.000000e+00         0   120     7     0     4 1.000000e+00
## 187:   IQCN   KRAS 1.000000e+00         0   120     7     0     4 1.000000e+00
## 188:   PAN2   KRAS 1.000000e+00         0   120     7     0     4 1.000000e+00
## 189:  PRPF8   KRAS 1.000000e+00         0   120     7     0     4 1.000000e+00
## 190:   IDH1    TTN 1.000000e+00         0   120     7     0     4 1.000000e+00
##                   Event          pair event_ratio
##                  <char>        <char>      <char>
##   1:       Co_Occurence HIVEP3, KMT2D         3/0
##   2:       Co_Occurence     PAN2, TTN         4/3
##   3:       Co_Occurence  HIVEP3, PAN2         3/1
##   4:       Co_Occurence   KMT2D, PAN2         3/1
##   5:       Co_Occurence   HIVEP3, TTN         3/4
##  ---                                             
## 186: Mutually_Exclusive    IDH1, KRAS        0/11
## 187: Mutually_Exclusive    IQCN, KRAS        0/11
## 188: Mutually_Exclusive    KRAS, PAN2        0/11
## 189: Mutually_Exclusive   KRAS, PRPF8        0/11
## 190: Mutually_Exclusive     IDH1, TTN        0/11

VAF Distribution

plotVaf(maf = maf, vafCol = "t_alt_count")

Interpretation

gs <- getGeneSummary(maf)
n_patients  <- getSampleSummary(maf) %>% nrow()
n_mutations <- nrow(maf@data)

cat(paste0(
  "### Key Findings\n\n",
  "- **Cohort size**: ", n_patients, " AML patients\n",
  "- **Total somatic mutations**: ", n_mutations, "\n",
  "- **Median mutations per patient**: ",
  median(getSampleSummary(maf)$total), "\n\n"
))

Key Findings

  • Cohort size: 131 AML patients
  • Total somatic mutations: 2912
  • Median mutations per patient: 5
cat("### Top mutated genes and their biological significance\n\n")

Top mutated genes and their biological significance

top_genes <- gs %>%
  arrange(desc(AlteredSamples)) %>%
  head(10)

annotations <- list(
  FLT3   = "Class I mutation — activates proliferation signalling. ~25% of AML. Targetable with midostaurin/gilteritinib.",
  NPM1   = "Class II mutation — causes nucleophosmin mislocalisation. ~30% of AML. Favourable prognosis when FLT3-ITD negative.",
  DNMT3A = "Epigenetic regulator — DNA methyltransferase. ~20% of AML. Often founding clonal event.",
  IDH1   = "Produces oncometabolite 2-HG, blocks differentiation. Targetable with ivosidenib.",
  IDH2   = "Same pathway as IDH1. Targetable with enasidenib.",
  TP53   = "Tumour suppressor — loss of function. Associated with complex karyotype and poor prognosis.",
  RUNX1  = "Transcription factor — core binding factor AML.",
  TET2   = "Epigenetic regulator — DNA demethylation. Associated with clonal haematopoiesis.",
  NRAS   = "RAS signalling — Class I proliferation mutation.",
  CEBPA  = "Transcription factor — biallelic mutations confer favourable prognosis."
)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(top_genes))) {
  gene <- top_genes$Hugo_Symbol[i]
  pct  <- round(top_genes$AlteredSamples[i] / n_patients * 100, 1)
  ann  <- annotations[[gene]]
  if (!is.null(ann)) {
    cat(paste0("- **", gene, "** (", pct, "% of patients): ", ann, "\n"))
  }
}
  • NPM1 (8.4% of patients): Class II mutation — causes nucleophosmin mislocalisation. ~30% of AML. Favourable prognosis when FLT3-ITD negative.
  • TP53 (6.9% of patients): Tumour suppressor — loss of function. Associated with complex karyotype and poor prognosis.
  • DNMT3A (6.1% of patients): Epigenetic regulator — DNA methyltransferase. ~20% of AML. Often founding clonal event.
  • RUNX1 (6.1% of patients): Transcription factor — core binding factor AML.
  • IDH2 (5.3% of patients): Same pathway as IDH1. Targetable with enasidenib.
  • FLT3 (4.6% of patients): Class I mutation — activates proliferation signalling. ~25% of AML. Targetable with midostaurin/gilteritinib.
